titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( Kirk ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( Kirk ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
2095. Despite serious misgivings concerning desirability and efficacy of retaliatory measures on comprehensive Soviet bloc basis suggested Sofia’s telegram 682 of August 16,Not printed; tot see footnote 4, p. 648. we would be prepared to consider this proposal further should Department decide to reopen subject (Deptel 7, January 5Not printed; but see Foreign Relations, 1948, vol. iv, footnote 2, p. 936.) retaliatory travel restrictions along lines Embtel 2735, November 26, 1948
Ibid., p. 934. in regard to Soviet personnel in the US. However, for time being “honeymoon” is still continuing here and I feel that the appreciable volume of useful information being obtained from the fairly numerous trips Soviets are approving to non-restricted areas contrary to predictions Embtel 2735 makes it highly inadvisable upset apple cart by instituting any retaliatory measures in absence further developments. Service Attachés concur.
Sent Dept 2095, repeated Sofia 40, Belgrade 86, Budapest 44, Bucharest 32.
